sanskrit:
न मां दुष्कृतिनो मूढा: प्रपद्यन्ते नराधमा: |
माययापहृतज्ञाना आसुरं भावमाश्रिता: ||
transliteration:
na māṁ duṣkṛtino mūḍhāḥ prapadyante narādhamāḥ |
māyayāpahṛta-jñānā āsuraṁ bhāvam āśritāḥ ||
literal_meaning: Not / to Me / evil-doers / deluded / they surrender / lowest of humans / by illusion / stolen / knowledge / demonic / nature / having taken shelter
contextual_meaning: People whose core identity is fused with narcissistic defenses, grandiosity, and exploitative schemas cannot psychologically tolerate the act of surrender; their ego-protective system blocks the integrative experience of humility and trust.
psychological_state: Demonizing grandiosity: a brittle, hyper-compensatory self-structure that wards off vulnerability by externalizing blame and devaluing dependency.
trigger_scenario: A high-achieving executive refuses coaching or therapy, insisting, 'I didn’t get here by listening to gurus,' while burnout and broken relationships mount.
anchor_text: Those clinging to ego cannot bow; surrender needs humility.
